title: Color Script
body:
Color Script is an ongoing painting system based on a limited palette, spatial relationships, and the interaction of color forms.

Each work is created using only five colors. This restriction is not a limitation but a framework that allows color to become an active structure rather than a descriptive tool.

The paintings do not begin with a predefined image. Forms emerge through the process itself, guided by movement, tension, contrast, and balance. Color fields collide, absorb, resist, or remain indifferent to one another, creating temporary states rather than fixed representations.

The works exist on the boundary between abstraction and memory. Viewers often recognize traces of landscapes, horizons, passages, or places, yet the image resists a clear identification. It feels familiar, but cannot be fully recalled.

A recurring vertical color code accompanies each painting. Extracted from the work itself, it functions as both a signature and a compressed fragment of the image. The color code is not an addition to the painting but part of its structure.

Individual works are connected through series and fragments. A painting can be seen as a single event, while a series reveals a larger visual language unfolding over time.

Color Script is not intended to illustrate stories, symbols, or spiritual concepts. It is an attempt to observe how color, form, and space behave when allowed to develop their own relationships.

Painting becomes a way of recording these encounters.
